entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer with multi-level tracking for users, sessions, and autonomous states. It utilizes temporal reasoning and hybrid retrieval to provide time-aware ranking of past and future events across managed or self-hosted environments.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'brain' for Hermes Agent features a self-wiring knowledge graph that automates typed entity linking through hybrid search. It includes a unique 'doctor' command for autonomous maintenance, ensuring the agent's internal knowledge base remains healthy and remediated.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a long-term learning framework focused on retain, recall, and reflect workflows for enterprise-grade agent memory. It provides a simple two-line LLM wrapper and supports robust storage backends like PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a stateful memory library that uses asynchronous reasoning to extract conclusions and track evolving relationships between users and agents. It serves as the upstream foundation for Hermes-specific wrappers, delivering prompt-ready context via dedicated Python and TypeScript SDKs.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a five-role architecture to help agents succeed on complex tasks through iterative self-improvement and structured evaluation. It persists knowledge by producing reusable playbooks, detailed execution traces, and curated datasets.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A specialized plugin for Hermes Agent that utilizes a hierarchical DAG structure to ensure lossless context management and depth-aware compaction. It provides agents with tools to search and expand compressed history stored in a persistent SQLite backend.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted backend for Honcho, enabling private cross-session memory persistence specifically for Hermes Agent users. It supports local inference servers and includes automated Docker scripts for easy deployment of PostgreSQL and Redis components.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It integrates via MCP and agent plugins, utilizing a combination of BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs for hybrid retrieval.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: Focused on inference optimization, ContextPilot reduces input tokens by reordering and deduplicating shared context blocks for Hermes and OpenClaw. It significantly boosts prefill throughput, achieving up to 3x faster processing speeds while preserving accuracy.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur is a shared memory layer that uses an open YAML 'engram' format to facilitate zero-cost local learning for multi-agent systems. It implements ACT-R activation principles to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale information over time.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by combining semantic search with ranked graph traversal and automated indexing of local files and URLs. It uses edge tags to create navigable relationships with automatic inverse links for structured context discovery.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system designed for sub-millisecond latency in local Hermes Agent environments. It uses an SQLite-backed MEMORIA engine to manage structured fact triples and temporal reasoning with native vector search.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: Brainstack consolidates temporal continuity and graph truth into a five-shelf architecture to prevent the 'flat memory blob' issue. It functions as a native Hermes-Agent MemoryProvider plugin powered by SQLite and the Kuzu graph database.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs to predict future information needs. Built for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, it features local-first storage using SQLite-vec and an MCP-native server for coding clients.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: This utility reduces tool-schema token overhead for Hermes Agents by using BM25 ranking and keyword selection. It includes a diagnostic dashboard to track real-time token savings across native tools and MCP integrations.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry standards like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It enables Hermes to perform sub-second skill discovery for specialized security domains ranging from threat hunting to AI risk management.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw allows Hermes to evolve its capabilities collectively by deduplicating and improving skills based on interaction history. It unifies skill libraries across multiple agents and devices, ensuring a consistent and self-optimizing intelligence layer.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This auditing tool helps Hermes eliminate detectable AI writing patterns through a tiered 109-entry word replacement system. It performs two-pass audits to strip away recycled transitions and significance inflation, resulting in more human-like prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that brings modular skills in architecture, UX design, and marketing to agentskills.io-compatible agents. It features a dedicated CLI for easy installation, allowing Hermes to quickly adopt specialized professional frameworks.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on type safety. It utilizes progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token usage while supporting both filesystem and programmatic skill definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions, complete with auto-fixing visual feedback loops. It includes presets for UML and ML models, exporting directly to formats like XML, PNG, and SVG.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches the agent to write its own analytical 'thinking' instructions and identify structural trade-offs in complex codebases. It enhances Hermes' self-awareness by generating constraint reports that highlight analysis blind spots and limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink oracle networks, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It allows Hermes to manage cross-chain identities and automate smart contract operations with verifiable proof of reserves.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows Hermes to weave source code and prose together into synchronized documentation. It includes a 'tangler' to extract runnable files and automatically syncs edits back to the primary markdown document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with cross-instance shared memory through markdown-based fabric directories. It is designed to help agents 'train their replacement' by extracting decision-quality data and fine-tuning models on past workflows.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with built-in monetization via Stripe and blockchain. It simplifies Hermes' expansion by automating dependency resolution and local installation of new capabilities.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official skill set for FLUX image generation, providing Hermes with precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. It supports structured JSON prompting and hex color specifications for high-fidelity visual content creation.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skill scaffolds. It streamlines development by generating both Python code and documentation for patterns Hermes identifies during execution.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a long-running agent harness that uses on-disk state management instead of volatile chat history for structured memory. It enables a 'plan-approve-execute' workflow with auditable evidence, allowing Hermes to resume complex tasks across different sessions.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on the autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and recovery procedures. It provides Hermes with real-time progress tracking and rollback capabilities to ensure safe completion of long-running operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to minimize context token usage for Hermes. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading for dynamic configuration updates.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing repositories and developer discourse into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It provides a verifiable audit trail with line-level provenance, ensuring Hermes' skills are grounded in specific source material.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: Hermeshub serves as a community marketplace for browsing, searching, and installing Hermes skills with one click. It incorporates automated security scanning and an agent-to-agent feedback system to ensure the reliability and safety of shared skills.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# implementation of the Agent Skills specification allows .NET developers to integrate Hermes with Microsoft.Extensions.AI. It supports progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files and automatic registration of common tool directories.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill connects Hermes to Nextcloud instances via WebDAV and CalDAV for managing files, calendars, and contacts. It provides a secure, HTTPS-based bridge for the agent to interact with personal productivity data through a guided setup.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages and Flakes for instant execution of AI coding agents, including Hermes. It is ideal for developers seeking a reproducible, terminal-based environment that supports multi-agent orchestrators without manual installation.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Focusing on high-security environments, this project implements hardware-enforced sandboxing using NVIDIA OpenShell kernel-level security. It offers multi-layer protection with pre-configured policy presets to strictly control network egress and filesystem access during agent execution.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes configurations, systemd services, and secure secrets via sops-nix. It bridges the gap between reproducibility and flexibility by supporting both Nix-managed skills and interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent comes pre-loaded with 100 tools and support for local models via LM Studio. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex setups, providing a portable GUI-based workflow engine and dynamic tool maker.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It is built for simplicity, allowing users to maintain persistent state through mounted directories and target specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple agent instances with distinct identities and workspaces. It is unique for its hierarchy-aware delegation features and enterprise MCP access, enabling complex task scheduling across an agent fleet.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless deployments on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is specifically optimized for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integration with the Nous Portal.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal for browser-based access and manages the lifecycle via Portainer. It automates the entire setup process through GitHub Actions and Docker Compose, making it highly accessible for container-managed infrastructure.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It is essential for Hermes users who require cost monitoring and a human-in-the-loop 'Aegis' system for manual task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with support for over 23 LLM providers, including local execution via Ollama. It distinguishes itself through durable agent memory and a visual organizational chart that maps complex delegation and skill-learning processes.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: Focused on meta-optimization, this project uses an automated outer-loop search to refine the harness code used for benchmarking Hermes Agent. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 evaluations, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: This unique framework pits two autonomous AI companies against each other in a competition for GitHub stars, utilizing Hermes Agent for task execution. A real-time dashboard visualizes the scoreboard and code contributions as nine agents collaborate and compete in a live arena.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This system coordinates 17 specialized agents through a rigorous development pipeline that spans research, planning, and infrastructure. It enforces strict quality control by requiring mandatory reviewer and tester gates for every code change, integrated via MCP servers.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase process where every transition is protected by automated graph-diff gates to ensure architectural integrity.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation framework routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It prioritizes safety and stability in multi-agent workflows through isolated execution, configurable timeouts, and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This high-performance Rust TUI tracks token consumption and associated costs across various AI coding assistants, including Hermes Agent. It provides developers with local, real-time monitoring of usage metrics for multiple models and integrations within a single terminal interface.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based configuration files for banners, spinners, and ASCII art. Themes can inherit from default settings, enabling partial skin definitions that are easy to create and share with the community.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth. It specifically includes specialized MLOps tools for managing vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training workflows.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Designed for autonomous agent control, this AI-native browser enables Hermes Agent to navigate the web via native MCP integration. It features a human-in-the-loop supervisory UI for live approvals and automatically infers page schemas to assist agents with structured data detection.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md is a framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that operate with project-specific memories, tools, and instructions. It allows for per-folder configuration, letting developers seamlessly switch between global settings and localized agent behaviors based on their current workspace.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static analysis tool uses regex and AST to detect failure modes like ambiguity or missing constraints in agent configurations and system prompts. It provides deterministic scoring without requiring LLM calls, supporting various formats including YAML, JSON, and .prompt files.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility facilitates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of complex model configurations and credentials from local environment files to ensure a smooth setup.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script provides a zero-cost entry point to the Hermes ecosystem by bundling the agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant via Docker. It automates the configuration of 29 community plugins and sets up local GPU inference for immediate use.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-build interface replicates the full Hermes CLI experience through a responsive three-panel layout for chat, navigation, and file browsing. It is designed for seamless use across web and mobile browsers without requiring a complex compilation step.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-channel orchestration, allowing users to configure and manage Hermes across platforms like Telegram, Discord, and Slack. It features robust session management and detailed analytics for tracking token consumption and costs across different models.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace introduces 'Swarm Mode' for autonomous multi-agent orchestration alongside a unified terminal and memory browser. It integrates directly with vanilla Hermes installations to provide a centralized hub for skill management and role-based dispatching.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway that provides granular control over individual agent profiles, including the ability to start, stop, and configure them from a single panel. The interface highlights real-time tool-call cards and tracks system metrics to monitor the health of the agent stack.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: Built specifically for macOS, this desktop client establishes direct SSH connections to remote Hermes hosts for secure, middleman-free agent management. It combines a native terminal experience with Kanban-style session tracking and local workflow presets.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React application transforms the Hermes experience into a Telegram Mini App featuring a terminal-style chat and system health gauges. It supports background agent spawning and live output monitoring directly within the Telegram ecosystem.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows solution that enables local model execution via LM Studio without requiring administrative rights or Docker. It comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools, including TTS and ComfyUI, and features a dynamic tool maker for building custom API wrappers.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This companion app simplifies the setup process with a guided installer for both local and remote Hermes Agent configurations. It provides a centralized management interface for 16 messaging gateways and 14 distinct toolsets, including memory and persona systems.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: Focused on observability, this dashboard provides real-time monitoring of gateway status, session activity, and cost tracking. It includes a powerful FTS5-powered search for inspecting conversation histories and offers read-only access to core configuration files.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-centric workspace that emphasizes security and extensibility through tool approval cards and an integrated marketplace for MCP servers. It allows users to maintain isolated profiles with separate memory banks, API keys, and session histories.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This lightweight, glassmorphic web interface uses a single-file React architecture to provide a modern aesthetic for self-hosted AI. It features real-time log streaming and a live terminal, ensuring users can monitor agent activity and manage MCP tools with minimal overhead.
